title: Production Ready Frontend
subtitle: Refactor a previous project for production — structure, hooks, performance, accessibility, and senior architecture explanations
prerequisites: week1 — components, props, useState, forms, routing fundamentals
week2 — APIs, custom hooks, Context, dynamic routes, machine coding Round 2
week3 — Redux Toolkit, RTK Query, authentication, performance, dashboards
day22 — machine coding interview, debugging drills, code review, 20 interview questions
expectedOutcome: Refactor one previous FrontForge project to production-ready standards
Apply React.memo, useMemo, useCallback, lazy loading, and code splitting only where measured and appropriate
Explain every architectural decision: folder structure, Context vs Redux vs RTK Query vs Router
Answer senior-level interview questions on architecture, performance, scalability, and maintainability
Document trade-offs and future improvements like a staff engineer in a design review
definitionOfDone: One previous project selected and refactored with improved folder structure
Reusable components and custom hooks extracted where duplication existed
Loading, error, and empty states verified on all async flows
Accessibility pass: keyboard navigation, labels, semantic HTML, focus management
TypeScript strict — no any, typed props and API models throughout
Performance checklist completed with written justification for each applied optimization
Architecture discussion document: why this structure, why each state solution, trade-offs, future improvements
8 senior interview questions answered out loud
Reflection submitted and refactored code pushed to Git

tomorrowPreview.title: Mock Frontend Interview
tomorrowPreview.summary: Final day — five timed interview rounds (JavaScript, React, machine coding, project discussion, debugging), self-evaluation rubric, graduation checklist, and you are done.
